name: Sea Salt / Himalayan Salt
description: Minimally processed sodium chloride with trace minerals, used in oral care, wound healing, saline solutions, and nasal irrigation via its osmotic antibacterial properties.
appearance: White, gray, pink, or reddish crystalline solid depending on source and mineral content
solubility: 360 g/L in water at 20°C; insoluble in ethanol
chemistry: Sodium chloride with trace minerals. Antibacterial via osmotic pressure — draws water out of bacterial cells. Promotes gum tissue healing.
warnings: Use fine-ground for toothpaste. Non-iodized for nasal irrigation.
